author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
When Perseverance is Granted to a Person, He Cannot But Persevere.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: When Perseverance is Granted to a Person, He Cannot But Persevere.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Now, moreover, when the saints say, “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil,” what do they pray for but that they may persevere in holiness?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For, assuredly, when that gift of God is granted to them,—which is sufficiently plainly shown to be God’s gift, since it is asked of Him,—that gift of God, then, being granted to them that they may not be led into temptation, none of the saints fails to keep his perseverance in holiness even to the end.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For there is not any one who ceases to persevere in the Christian purpose unless he is first of all led into temptation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: If, therefore, it be granted to him according to his prayer that he may not be led, certainly by the gift of God he persists in that sanctification which by the gift of God he has received.
